mount_everest.definition: The highest mountain in the world.
mount_everest.location: Himalayas, on the border of Nepal and China (Tibet).
mount_everest.importance: Attracts climbers worldwide; important for climate and geological studies.
mount_everest.fun_fact: Its height is 8,848 meters (29,029 ft) above sea level.

nile_river.definition: The longest river in the world.
nile_river.location: Flows through northeastern Africa, mainly Egypt and Sudan.
nile_river.importance: Supports agriculture, water supply, and human settlements.
nile_river.fun_fact: It is over 6,650 km (4,130 miles) long.

amazon_river.definition: The second longest river, largest by discharge volume.
amazon_river.location: South America, mainly Brazil and Peru.
amazon_river.importance: Crucial for rainforest ecosystem and biodiversity.
amazon_river.fun_fact: It has more water than the next seven largest rivers combined.

sahara_desert.definition: The largest hot desert in the world.
sahara_desert.importance: Affects climate, human settlement, and ecosystems.
sahara_desert.fun_fact: It is roughly the size of the United States.

great_barrier_reef.definition: The world's largest coral reef system.
great_barrier_reef.importance: Supports marine biodiversity, tourism, and coastal protection.

grand_canyon.definition: A massive canyon carved by the Colorado River.
grand_canyon.importance: Geological study, tourism, and cultural significance.
grand_canyon.fun_fact: It is over 446 km (277 miles) long.

mt_fuji.definition: A famous stratovolcano and highest peak in Japan.
mt_fuji.importance: Cultural symbol, hiking destination, geological studies.

sahara_oasis.definition: Fertile areas in a desert with water sources.
sahara_oasis.location: Various locations across the Sahara Desert.
sahara_oasis.importance: Supports human settlements and agriculture in deserts.
sahara_oasis.fun_fact: Some oases are hundreds of years old.

andes_mountains.location: Western South America, across seven countries.
andes_mountains.importance: Geography, climate influence, minerals, and biodiversity.
andes_mountains.fun_fact: Home to the highest volcanoes in the world.

volga_river.fun_fact: It drains into the Caspian Sea.

dead_sea.definition: A salt lake with extremely high salinity.
dead_sea.location: Border of Jordan, Israel, and Palestine.
dead_sea.fun_fact: You can float easily because of the high salt content.

lake_superior.definition: The largest of the Great Lakes of North America.
lake_superior.fun_fact: It has the largest surface area of any freshwater lake in the world.

mississippi_river.definition: One of the longest rivers in North America.
mississippi_river.location: United States, flowing from Minnesota to the Gulf of Mexico.

everglades.importance: Biodiversity hotspot, water regulation, flood control.

siberia.definition: A vast region of Russia covering most of northern Asia.
siberia.location: Russia, from the Ural Mountains to the Pacific.
siberia.importance: Natural resources, wildlife, extreme climate studies.
siberia.fun_fact: Home to permafrost and the Siberian tiger.

great_lakes.definition: A group of five interconnected freshwater lakes.
great_lakes.fun_fact: Contain 21% of the world's surface freshwater.

rocky_mountains.definition: Major mountain range in North America.
rocky_mountains.location: From Canada to the USA (New Mexico).
rocky_mountains.importance: Tourism, biodiversity, minerals, and climate influence.
rocky_mountains.fun_fact: Home to grizzly bears, moose, and mountain goats.

alps.definition: A major mountain range in Europe.
alps.location: Across eight countries including France, Switzerland, Italy, and Austria.
alps.importance: Tourism, skiing, culture, and climate studies.
alps.fun_fact: Mont Blanc is the highest peak at 4,808 meters.

ganges_river.definition: A major river in South Asia.
ganges_river.importance: Religious significance, agriculture, and water supply.
